messages.content: I am getting the jump action in other columns even though i have this statement; If Target.Column = 1 Then GoTo Jump

As an AI language model, I cannot see the entire code and understand the context of the statement, but here are some possible reasons why the jump action is happening in other columns despite the conditional statement:

- The Jump label is not only called by Target.Column = 1 condition but is also called by other conditions or events.
- There might be other code statements that modify the behavior of the program outside of the conditional statements.
- The GoTo statement could be causing issues in the program flow, leading to unexpected results.

To solve this issue, you can modify the Jump label or the code flow to ensure that it only runs under the Target.Column = 1 condition. Alternatively, you can consider using other programming techniques such as If-else statement or Select Case statement to handle multiple conditions in a more structured and predictable manner.
i only want the GoTo to be called by activity in column 1 and no other column

If the jump action is happening in other columns despite the conditional statement "If Target.Column = 1 Then GoTo Jump", it is possible that the code is not properly accounting for all the possible events or conditions that can cause the jump action to happen.

You can try to modify the code to handle the event only when it occurs in column 1. Here is an example of how to modify the code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        ' Only jump if the change occurs in column 1
        GoTo Jump
    End If
    ' Other code statements for other events or conditions in other columns
    ' ...
    Exit Sub ' Exit the sub if no jump action is triggered
Jump:
    ' Code statements for the jump action
End Sub
```

In this modified code, the jump action will only be triggered when there is a change in column 1. If the change occurs in any other column, the code will exit the sub and not execute the jump action. 

Hope this helps!
This code is not stopping other columns from going to jump. Can you please detect and show me the issue

Sure, I can help you identify the issue.

Here is an example of how to modify the code to ensure that the jump action is only triggered when a change occurs in column 1:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the Target range is in column 1
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        ' Code statements for the jump action
        GoTo Jump
    Else
        Exit Sub ' Exit the sub if the change is not in column 1
    End If
Jump:
    ' Code statements for the jump action
End Sub
```

In this modified code, the sub checks whether the Target range is in column 1, and only executes the code statements for the jump action if it is in column 1. If the target range is in any other column, the sub will exit immediately and not execute the jump action.

I hope this helps you solve the issue. Let me know if you have any further questions or concerns!

here is what my code looks like; Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
Dim wsd As Worksheet

If Target.Column = 1 Then
Application.Wait Now + TimeValue("00:00:01")
GoTo Jump
End If

If Target.Address = "$H$2" Then
If Module9.ProvidersA380 = False Then Exit Sub
MsgBox ("H2A")
Range("H3:H80").Calculate
Module9.ProvidersA380 = True
End If

If Target.Column = 4 Then
If Len(Target.Value) > 3 Then
Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Left(Target.Value, 3))
wsd.Activate
Else
Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Target.Value)
wsd.Activate
End If
Exit Sub

Jump:

Based on the code snippet you have provided, it seems that the conditional statement is only checking for whether Target.Column is equal to 1, and is executing the "GoTo Jump" statement if it is true. However, the subsequent code statements do not have any conditionals, which means that they will be executed regardless of the column selected.

If you want to ensure that the jump action is only triggered when a change occurs in column 1, you can modify the code as follows:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        Application.Wait Now + TimeValue("00:00:01")
        GoTo Jump
    End If
    
    ' Add conditional statements for other columns here
    If Target.Address = "$H$2" Then
        If Module9.ProvidersA380 = False Then Exit Sub
        MsgBox ("H2A")
        Range("H3:H80").Calculate
        Module9.ProvidersA380 = True
    End If
    
    If Target.Column = 4 Then
        If Len(Target.Value) > 3 Then
            Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Left(Target.Value, 3))
            wsd.Activate
        Else
            Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Target.Value)
            wsd.Activate
        End If
    End If ' End of conditional statements for other columns
    
    Exit Sub
    
Jump:
    ' Code statements for the jump action
End Sub
```

In this modified code, the "GoTo Jump" statement will only be triggered when a selection change occurs in column 1. The subsequent code statements will only execute if the selection is in another column (e.g. column 4), and will skip the jump action.

I hope this helps you solve the issue. Let me know if you have any further questions or concerns!
Thanks it worked
You're welcome! I'm glad it worked. If you have any further questions or concerns, feel free to ask.
How can I use a boolean  true or false to control a code

You can use a Boolean variable to control a code in VBA. A Boolean variable can have two values, either "True" or "False", and is often used to store a condition or a state.

Here is an example of how you can use a Boolean variable to control a code:

```
Private Sub MySub()
    Dim myBoolean As Boolean
    myBoolean = True
    
    If myBoolean = True Then
        ' Code to execute when the Boolean is True
        MsgBox "The Boolean is True!"
    Else
        ' Code to execute when the Boolean is False
        MsgBox "The Boolean is False!"
    End If
End Sub
```

In this example, we declare a Boolean variable called "myBoolean" and assign it a value of True. We then use an "If" statement to check if the Boolean is True. If it is True, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box that says "The Boolean is True!"). If it is False, we execute some other code (in this case, we display a message box that says "The Boolean is False!").

You can modify the code inside the "If" and "Else" statements to perform any action you need. You can also change the value of the Boolean variable to control the execution of the code.

I hope this helps!
Can I put this code in the same sheet where I will use it or does it have to be in a module

You can put the code in the same sheet where you will use it. In VBA, you can write event procedures that are associated with a particular worksheet or workbook. The event procedure will automatically run when the event is triggered. 

Here's an example of how to use a boolean variable to control a code in the Worksheet_Change event procedure of a specific sheet:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim myBoolean As Boolean
    myBoolean = False
    
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        myBoolean = True
    End If
    
    If myBoolean = True Then
        ' Code to execute when the Boolean is True
        MsgBox "The Boolean is True and Target is in column 1!"
    Else
        ' Code to execute when the Boolean is False or Target is not in column 1
        MsgBox "The Boolean is False or Target is not in column 1!"
    End If
End Sub
```

In this example, we declare a Boolean variable called "myBoolean" and assign it a value of False. We then check if the Target range is in column 1. If it is in column 1, we set the Boolean variable to True. Lastly, we use an "If" statement to check if the Boolean variable is True. If it is True, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box that says "The Boolean is True and Target is in column 1!"). If it is False, we execute some other code (in this case, we display a message box that says "The Boolean is False or Target is not in column 1!").

You can modify the code inside the "If" and "Else" statements to perform any action you need. You can also change the value of the Boolean variable to control the execution of the code.

Note that this code should be placed in the code module for the worksheet where you want to use it. To do so, right-click the sheet name tab, select "View Code" from the context menu, and paste the code into the code module that appears.

It does not appear as if Target.Address works;  If Target.Address = "$H$2" Then
If H380 = True Then
MsgBox ("Yes Calculate")
Range("H3:H80").Calculate
Else
MsgBox ("No Calculate")
End If

It looks like the issue might be that the Target.Address property is returning an absolute address, whereas the string "$H$2" is a relative address. To compare the Target's address to a relative address, you can use the Target's "AddressLocal" property instead. Here's an example:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Compare the AddressLocal property to the relative reference "H2"
    If Target.AddressLocal = "H2" Then
        If H380 = True Then ' Assuming that H380 is a boolean variable in your code
            MsgBox ("Yes Calculate")
            Range("H3:H80").Calculate
        Else
            MsgBox ("No Calculate")
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this example, we use the AddressLocal property to compare the Target range's address to the relative reference "H2". If the two are the same, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box and then calculate the range H3:H80). If the Target range is not in cell H2, the code does nothing.

I hope this helps!
If Target.AddressLocal = "H2" Then . . . Target.AddressLocal does not work

I apologize for the confusion. Based on your previous response, it looks like you are using the Worksheet_SelectionChange event instead of Worksheet_Change. That's why Target.AddressLocal is not working - it is not a property of the Target range object for the SelectionChange event.

For the SelectionChange event, you can use the Target.Address property, which returns the absolute address of the selected cell. You can then compare it to the string "$H$2" to check if the selection has changed to that cell. Here is an example:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Address = "$H$2" Then
        If H380 = True Then
            MsgBox ("Yes Calculate")
            Range("H3:H80").Calculate
        Else
            MsgBox ("No Calculate")
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this example, we check if the Target range's address is "$H$2". If it is, we execute some code depending on the value of the H380 variable.

I apologize for any confusion caused by my previous response. Please let me know if you have any further questions or concerns!

Unfortunately changes to the Boolean Yes or False cannot be read in this location of the same sheet.

MsgBox ("Yes Calculate")

Jump:
A380 = False
H380 = False

I am trying to do a range recalculation only once when the page is activated and not continuously when the page is open.

Any suggestions

Based on the code you have provided, it looks like the issue might be that the Boolean variables A380 and H380 are being declared and assigned values in the Worksheet_Activate event, but are not declared and assigned a value in the Worksheet_SelectionChange event, which is why the code cannot read their values.

One way to resolve this issue is to declare the Boolean variables at the module level, so that they can be used across different procedures within the same code module. Here's an example:

```
Option Explicit ' This line enforces variable declaration in this code module

Dim A380 As Boolean ' Declare the Boolean variable at the module level
Dim H380 As Boolean ' Declare the Boolean variable at the module level

Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Application.Calculation = xlManual
    Application.EnableEvents = True
    A380 = True ' Assign a value to the Boolean variable
    H380 = True ' Assign a value to the Boolean variable
End Sub

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim wsd As Worksheet
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        If A380 = True Then
            MsgBox ("Yes Calculate")
            Range("A3:A80").Calculate
            A380 = False ' Update the Boolean variable value
        Else
            MsgBox ("No Calculate")
        End If
    End If
    
    ' Code for other Target columns goes here
    
End Sub
```

In this example, we declare the Boolean variables A380 and H380 at the module level, so that they can be used across different procedures within the same code module. We assign values to the Boolean variables in the Worksheet_Activate event, and update the value of A380 in the Worksheet_SelectionChange event. 

I hope this helps you solve the issue! Let me know if you have any further questions or concerns.
Fantastic your suggestions worked
You're welcome! I'm glad that my suggestions worked for you. If you have any further questions or concerns, feel free to ask!
The only other thing I need to work is this line of code; If Target.Address = "H$2" Then   I cannot get the vba to recognise Address in Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange

You are correct that the Address property is not available in the Worksheet_SelectionChange event. Instead, you can use the Address property along with the worksheet's Name property to construct an absolute reference like "SheetName!H2", and compare that to the Target's Address property. Here's an example:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Address = Me.Name & "!$H$2" Then
        ' Code to execute when H2 is selected
        MsgBox "H2 is selected!"
    End If
End Sub
```

In this example, we first construct an absolute reference to cell H2 on the current sheet using the worksheet's Name property and the string "$H$2". Then, we compare that to the Target range's Address property. If the addresses match, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box).

Note that we use the Me keyword to refer to the current worksheet, and the "&" symbol to concatenate the worksheet's Name property with the string "$H$2".

I hope this helps you solve the issue! Let me know if you have any further questions or concerns.
The suggestion did not work

I apologize for the confusion in my previous response. The suggested solution did not work because the Name property returns the name of the worksheet, but not the sheet name as it appears in the tab of the worksheet.

To make the code work, you can use the code name of the worksheet instead of the sheet name. The code name is the internal name given to a sheet object when it is created, and it does not change even if the name of the worksheet as it appears on the tab is changed. The code name of a worksheet can be found in the Properties window in the Visual Basic Editor, and can be changed if needed.

Here's an example of how to use the code name of a worksheet in the Worksheet_SelectionChange event:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    
    If Target.Address = Range("H2").Address Then
        ' Code to execute when H2 is selected
        MsgBox "H2 is selected!"
    End If

End Sub
```

In this example, we compare the Target range's address to the address of the cell H2. If the addresses match, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box).

I hope this helps to resolve the issue!

In this example, we check if the Target range's address is equal to the string "$H$2". If it is, we execute some code (in this case, we display a message box).

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Cells.Count = 1 Then ' only trigger the code if one cell is selected
        If Not Intersect(Target, Me.Range("H2")) Is Nothing Then ' check if cell H2 is selected
            ' Code to execute when H2 is selected
            MsgBox "H2 is selected!"
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```
